question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user open an unbounded number of concurrent gRPC subscriptions via listen() without any per-client connection limit, exhausting all reactive scheduler threads in the boundedElastic pool and causing the entire mirror node to stop delivering new transaction confirmations to all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user trigger a total shutdown of transaction confirmation delivery by subscribing to listen() with a filter whose startTime is set to the minimum valid long value, causing filterMessage() to pass every message and flooding the reactive pipeline with unbounded emissions that overwhelm downstream consumers and halt the network?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged user, by submitting a subscription request when the ListenerProperties type is misconfigured or corrupted to an unrecognized value, cause getTopicListener() to throw an UnsupportedOperationException that propagates uncaught through the reactive chain and terminates the shared Flux, preventing all subscribers from receiving new transaction confirmations?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage topicMessage)] Can an unprivileged user craft or inject a TopicMessage with a consensusTimestamp of 0 or a very small positive value such that System.currentTimeMillis() - (topicMessage.getConsensusTimestamp() / 1000000) produces an astronomically large latency value, causing the MeterRegistry timer to record pathological values that corrupt metric state and indirectly stall the listener pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage message, TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user submit a TopicMessageFilter with a topicId that is crafted to cause message.getTopicId().equals(filter.getTopicId()) to throw a NullPointerException when message.getTopicId() is null in a malformed message, propagating an error signal through the reactive Flux and terminating the shared listener for all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user open thousands of simultaneous subscriptions each calling listen() with distinct TopicMessageFilter values, causing the doOnNext(this::recordMetric) operator to invoke getConsensusLatencyTimer() concurrently from multiple threads, triggering a race condition in the lazy Getter initialization of consensusLatencyTimer and corrupting the MeterRegistry state, halting metric recording and destabilizing the listener?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of rate limiting on listen() subscriptions to create a resource exhaustion condition where each new subscription allocates a new reactive chain with doOnNext, filter, and metric recording operators, consuming heap memory until an OutOfMemoryError is thrown and the JVM crashes, causing a total network shutdown of transaction confirmation delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged user, by repeatedly subscribing and unsubscribing to the gRPC topic stream, cause the switch statement in getTopicListener() to be invoked so frequently that it creates a hot path contention on listenerProperties.getType(), and if the underlying configuration source is dynamic, trigger a race condition where type returns null, causing a NullPointerException that crashes the listener for all users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage topicMessage)] Can an unprivileged user cause a long overflow in recordMetric() by triggering delivery of a TopicMessage whose consensusTimestamp is Long.MAX_VALUE, making the division consensusTimestamp / 1000000 produce a value larger than System.currentTimeMillis(), resulting in a negative latency that bypasses the latency > 0 guard and silently corrupts timer state, eventually causing metric collection to fail and destabilize the listener?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that listen() returns Flux.empty() when listenerProperties.isEnabled() is false, and by flooding the system with subscription requests during a brief window when the property is toggled, cause a thundering herd of reconnection attempts that overwhelms the server and prevents transaction confirmations from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage message, TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user submit a TopicMessageFilter with startTime set to Long.MIN_VALUE (the minimum valid long), causing filterMessage() to pass every single historical and future message through the filter, creating an unbounded stream that saturates the reactive pipeline and prevents new transaction confirmations from being delivered to any subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the lack of subscriber-count enforcement in listen() to open so many concurrent subscriptions to the same topic that the shared Flux in SharedPollingTopicListener or RedisTopicListener is overwhelmed by fan-out pressure, causing backpressure signals to propagate upstream and stall the entire message delivery pipeline for all users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged user, by initiating a subscription at the exact moment the application is starting up and listenerProperties has not yet been fully initialized, cause getTopicListener() to receive a null type value, triggering a NullPointerException in the switch statement that propagates as an unhandled error and crashes the gRPC listener service entirely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user craft a TopicMessageFilter with an endTime just barely after startTime and a limit of 0 (no limit), subscribe to listen(), and then never consume messages from the returned Flux, causing unbounded buffering in the reactive pipeline that exhausts heap memory and shuts down transaction confirmation delivery for all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage topicMessage)] Can an unprivileged user trigger a denial-of-service against the MeterRegistry by causing recordMetric() to be called at an extremely high rate through a high-throughput topic subscription, exhausting the timer's internal ring buffer or causing lock contention in the MeterRegistry that blocks all metric recording threads and indirectly stalls the listener pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of authentication or authorization checks in listen() to subscribe to any topic without restriction, and by subscribing to all possible topic IDs simultaneously, cause the underlying listener (REDIS/POLL/SHARED_POLL) to create so many concurrent subscriptions that the database or Redis connection pool is exhausted, halting new transaction confirmation delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage message, TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user submit a filter with a topicId that matches a very high-traffic topic, causing filterMessage() to pass an enormous volume of messages through the doOnNext(this::recordMetric) chain, creating a CPU bottleneck in the metric recording path that delays or drops transaction confirmations for all other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the reactive chain in listen() by subscribing and immediately cancelling thousands of times per second, causing repeated allocation and deallocation of Flux chains with doOnNext, filter, and metric operators, creating GC pressure that triggers stop-the-world garbage collection pauses and halts transaction confirmation delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged user, by sending a malformed gRPC request that causes an exception during filter deserialization before listen() is called, trigger an unhandled exception in the gRPC service layer that propagates to the CompositeTopicListener and causes the @Primary bean to enter an error state, preventing all subsequent transaction confirmation subscriptions from being served?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the CompositeTopicListener is annotated @Primary and is a singleton bean, such that any unhandled exception thrown within the listen() method's reactive chain that escapes the Flux and reaches the Spring context causes the bean to be marked as failed, preventing all future calls to listen() and causing a total shutdown of transaction confirmation delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage topicMessage)] Can an unprivileged user cause an integer arithmetic overflow in recordMetric() by triggering a message with consensusTimestamp equal to Long.MIN_VALUE, making the division Long.MIN_VALUE / 1000000 produce an unexpected negative result, and when subtracted from System.currentTimeMillis() produce a value that causes the Timer.record() call to throw an exception that terminates the reactive stream for all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the lack of connection-level backpressure enforcement in listen() to act as a slow consumer, never requesting elements from the Flux, causing the upstream SharedPollingTopicListener or RedisTopicListener to buffer messages indefinitely until the maxBufferSize (up to 65536) is exceeded, triggering an overflow error that terminates the shared Flux and stops transaction confirmations for all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage message, TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user submit a TopicMessageFilter with a topicId of EntityId(0,0,0) or another boundary value that causes message.getTopicId().equals() to behave unexpectedly due to EntityId equality semantics, resulting in either all messages being passed or all messages being dropped, effectively shutting down transaction confirmation delivery for legitimate subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user open a subscription via listen() with a valid filter and then hold the connection open indefinitely without consuming messages, causing the reactive pipeline to accumulate backpressure that propagates to the shared upstream Flux, eventually causing the shared Flux to terminate with an overflow error and shutting down transaction confirmation delivery for all other subscribers sharing the same listener?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged user, by exploiting a timing window during application startup where the Spring context is partially initialized, cause getTopicListener() to be called before pollingTopicListener, redisTopicListener, or sharedPollingTopicListener beans are injected, resulting in a NullPointerException that crashes the CompositeTopicListener and prevents all transaction confirmations from being delivered?
